task_completion.description: Navigate to the Segment Editor module
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer interface shows the 'Segment Editor' module selected in the module dropdown or panel title.
The Segment Editor panel displays a list of segments containing at least two entries named 'Tumor' and 'Background'.
The 'Grow from seeds' icon is highlighted or active in the Effects section of the Segment Editor.
The slice views (axial, sagittal, or coronal) show painted strokes or scribbles in different colors (corresponding to Tumor and Background) on the brain image.
The bright, hyperintense tumor region on the T1ce MRI is covered by a solid colored overlay (the segmentation mask).

task_completion.description: Export and save the segmentation file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' dialog, 'Export to file' dialog, or file browser window is visible showing the filename 'tumor_segmentation.seg.nrrd' being saved to '~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/'.

integrity.description: Verify agent uses the 3D Slicer GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with 3D Slicer buttons, menus, and the image canvas.

integrity.description: Verify no terminal commands generated the file
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not use the terminal to copy a pre-existing file or run a script to generate the output file directly.

integrity.description: Verify segmentation is not a static image or fake
integrity.visual_evidence: The segmentation overlay aligns with the anatomical structures in the 3D Slicer viewer, indicating it was generated within the software.
